bottleneck.evidence: Final evaluate.py repeat=3: removing the decode-impossible layout.item() branch reduced median latency from the live reference baseline to 75.97 us at M=16 and 98.27 us at M=32; outputs remained bit-identical. Advisory profile showed the GEMM itself is memory-bound with heavy padded work (useful_vs_padded_util 0.0034/0.0076), but the measured win came from avoiding a per-call device scalar read before launch.

approaches.summary: Removed the runtime layout.item() check and contiguous fallback from run(), directly calling the masked DeepGEMM path used by this decode-only workload.
approaches.why: The task sweep has layout=1 for every shape, so the branch only added a device scalar synchronization and Python dispatch overhead before the real DeepGEMM launch.

approaches.summary: Tried recipe=(1,128,128) to fp8_m_grouped_gemm_nt_masked to make the scale layout explicit.
approaches.why: The task inputs already carry TMA-transformed scale tensors; the explicit recipe made DeepGEMM reinterpret their layout and failed the scale-shape assertion.

approaches.summary: Ran integrate.py after the WIN and reproduced the same failure with a reference.py copy as the candidate.
approaches.why: The integration recipe compares separate uninitialized output buffers for empty experts that DeepGEMM intentionally leaves untouched; computed expert rows match, but empty rows contain unrelated memory.

lesson: For decode-only GLM52 grouped-MoE tasks, avoid reading scalar layout tensors inside run() when the workload sweep fixes layout=1. The saved device synchronization can be larger than the DeepGEMM kernel tuning headroom at tiny routed loads.
transfers_to: glm52 grouped-moe decode tasks with fixed masked layout
small-M routed expert GEMM wrappers that branch on device scalar metadata
caveats: Does not apply to prefill or mixed-layout tasks that genuinely need the contiguous path
The current grouped-MoE integration verifier can fail on empty experts because reference and candidate outputs leave separate uninitialized buffers untouched
